question: A developer renames a DMN decision in the Camunda Modeler from `Dish` to `Course`. Looking at the XML below, where is the human-readable decision name stored?

options.text: As the `name` attribute on the `<decision>` element
As the `name` attribute on the `<decisionTable>` element
As the `id` attribute on the `<decision>` element
As a `<name>` child element nested inside `<decision>`

a.text: The decision name describes the decision and is set as the `name` attribute on the `decision` element. It can also be changed via the Properties Panel in the DRD view.

b.text: The `decisionTable` element carries its own `id` and the `hitPolicy` attribute, but the human-readable decision name lives on the parent `decision` element, not on `decisionTable`.

c.text: The `id` attribute is the technical identifier of the decision, not its human-readable name. Both attributes can coexist on the same `decision` element.

d.text: DMN stores the decision name as an XML attribute on `decision`, not as a child element.

explanation: The decision name describes the decision and is set as the `name` attribute on the `decision` element. It can be changed via the Properties Panel after selecting the Decision in the DRD view; the technical `id` is a separate attribute on the same element.
